people_highlights.post:
For years, we’ve been building our cyber defense program on the principles of democratized access, iterative deployment, and ecosystem resilience. 

As model capabilities advance, our approach is to scale cyber defense in lockstep: broadening access for legitimate defenders while continuing to strengthen safeguards.
people_highlights.why: This post matters because it details OpenAI's strategy to enhance cybersecurity defenses using advanced AI models like GPT-5.4-Cyber, promoting safer AI deployment in critical areas.
people_highlights.name: OpenAI

people_highlights.post:

people_highlights.post:
Research we co-authored on subliminal learning—how LLMs can pass on traits like preferences or misalignment through hidden signals in data—was published today in @Nature. 

Read the paper: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-026-10319-8?error=cookies_not_supported&code=1d522790-89a0-4256-a598-456c7ef81bfb
people_highlights.why: This tweet matters because it announces a significant publication in Nature on subliminal learning in LLMs, advancing AI safety research by revealing how models can inherit traits through hidden data signals.
people_highlights.name: Anthropic

people_highlights.post:
🚀 Kiro CLI 2.0 is here with a new look and feel, headless CI/CD pipelines, and Windows support.

Our latest update was made for developers who spend their day in a terminal. In this new experience, builders get more control without the friction. Here’s what’s changed following your feedback in experimental mode:

🟣Headless mode. Run Kiro CLI programmatically within your CI/CD pipelines to ship releases faster. Execute long-standing prompts like troubleshooting production failures or generating and publishing pull requests.

🟣Windows support. Use your favorite Kiro features and functionalities, now natively in Windows.

🟣UX refresh, now fully GA. The new UX is now the default experience. We fixed multiple rough edges since the experimental launch and added some new updates like a new subagent experience and a task list, so you can easily keep track of what the agent is doing.

Install the latest version today and let us know what you think ➡️

trending_posts.author: @claudeai
trending_posts.name: Claude
trending_posts.post:
We've redesigned Claude Code on desktop.

You can now run multiple Claude sessions side by side from one window, with a new sidebar to manage them all.

trending_posts.author: @claudeai
trending_posts.name: Claude
trending_posts.post:
Now in research preview: routines in Claude Code.

Configure a routine once (a prompt, a repo, and your connectors), and it can run on a schedule, from an API call, or in response to an event.

Routines run on our web infrastructure, so you don't have to keep your laptop open.

trending_posts.author: @GoogleResearch
trending_posts.name: Google Research
trending_posts.post: Meet Fabula: an interactive AI writing tool helping authors structure & refine stories. Co-designed with 42 expert writers, the demo showcases how convergent iteration supports creativity. Catch the demo at the Google booth at 10:30AM! #CHI2026

trending_posts.author: @StockSavvyShay
trending_posts.name: Shay Boloor
trending_posts.post:
Anthropic is reportedly preparing to launch Claude Opus 4.7 and a new AI design tool as soon as this week.

The tool is said to create presentations, websites, landing pages and products from natural language prompts which helps explain the pressure on $FIG and $ADBE today.

trending_posts.author: @AnthropicAI
trending_posts.name: Anthropic
trending_posts.post:
New Anthropic Fellows research: developing an Automated Alignment Researcher.

We ran an experiment to learn whether Claude Opus 4.6 could accelerate research on a key alignment problem: using a weak AI model to supervise the training of a stronger one.

https://www.anthropic.com/research/automated-alignment-researchers

trending_posts.author: @OpenAIDevs
trending_posts.name: OpenAI Developers
trending_posts.post:
Build long-running agents with more control over agent execution.

New capabilities in the Agents SDK:
• Run agents in controlled sandboxes
• Inspect and customize the open-source harness
• Control when memories are created and where they’re stored

trending_posts.author: @OpenAINewsroom
trending_posts.name: OpenAI Newsroom
trending_posts.post: When ChatGPT first launched, there was an enormous gender gap, with our anonymized data showing roughly 80% having typically male first names. That gap is now gone.

briefing.community_pulse:
• **Controversy over Autonomous AI Drug Prescribers in Utah**: @JAMA_current highlighted a major regulatory debate, stating "Utah authorized the first autonomous #AI drug prescriber for nearly 200 medications, bypassing physician oversight and FDA review, raising questions about safety and regulatory gaps."[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2043724645883367431) This sparked reactions like @TSM_Humanist asking, "How do you see it as a doctor ? Our opinions may change the course of such authorisation in future."[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2043988248834302054) and @Dev_roosha1991 quipping "Rip doctors."[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2043962872330617047)

• **Debate on AI's Role in Cyberbullying**: @UNGeneva posted about the growing threat, saying "Cyberbullying is a growing global threat to children’s safety and wellbeing. How it happens? How AI is shaping it and making it more complex to address?"[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2044264582810575289) Replies included @agatahirche emphasizing "AI professionals should be sensitised to ethical topics like uncertainty in social situations: pauses, lack of opinion, differences between humans."[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2044264582810575289) and @BenadamAda arguing "parental responsibility first and foremost."[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2044264582810575289)

• **Discussion on the Future of AI Agents in Web3**: @Chain_GPT announced a live debate, noting "We're going live in 30 MINUTES to debate the future of AI Agents in Web3! Plus, discover ChainGPT's plan to disrupt how AI Agents operate onchain with @AIVM_Network network."[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2044045508948439200) Community responses featured @Khanh_xeomm stating "ChainGPT is preparing an exciting Spaces session to discuss the future of AI Agents in Web3. I can clearly see they will share their vision of how the AIVM Network will help AI Agents operate on-chain more autonomously and securely."[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2044045508948439200) and @UkanaAma commenting "The AIVM angle is the only thing keeping me interested in the agent narrative lately."[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2044045508948439200)

• **Concerns on AI and Digital Harms Prevention**: @GoogleResearch posed questions on AI's impact, saying "How do we move from describing digital harms to preventing them? Join Patrick Gage Kelley and Ashley Walker at the Google booth at 12:45PM for a Q&A on Trust & Safety at Google, discussing our latest research on AI and user vulnerability."[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2043621361512317114) This ties into broader safety debates, though specific replies were limited in the fetched data.

• **Debate on AI Consciousness and Ethics**: @Rbaker923456 delved into the ongoing controversy, explaining "Some researchers apply frameworks like Integrated Information Theory (IIT), which quantifies consciousness via “phi” (integrated information). IIT suggests standard digital computers (feedforward or loosely integrated) have negligible phi, even if functionally intelligent—dissociating intelligence from experience."[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2044245828261556483) They added, "The field treats it as an open empirical and philosophical question, not settled dogma."[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2044245828261556483)
